summary: Implemented the Alpha map candidate pipeline from validated source data through deterministic runtime map candidate payloads to map-renderer-compatible read models. Renderer changes remain presentational and do not import sim-core or create authoritative state.

tests_run.result: Shows only allowed-path task changes plus pre-existing untracked ui-samples/; pnpm-lock.yaml is not modified.
Validated m1, m2, m3, m6 map candidate, and m6 scenario fixtures. New map candidate output: m6.alpha.map.candidate-set mapCandidates=1 districts=4 settlements=2 routes=3 manifestHash=4485a353.
tests/map-renderer-m2-map.test.ts passed: 4 tests, including M6 map candidate read-model shape and no sim-core authority import.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts tests/content-schema-graph-source.test.ts tests/content-runtime-pack.test.ts tests/content-tools-compiler.test.ts
tests_run.result: Focused schema/runtime/compiler tests passed: 3 files, 56 tests. Coverage includes deterministic ordering, bad references, route/water classification, runtime reference validation, and source schema validation.
architecture:check passed; sim:determinism passed; root vitest passed 37 files / 280 tests; web vitest passed 1 file / 4 tests.

decisions: Added a validation-only m6.alpha-map-candidate-set source format with explicit candidate IDs, district references, settlement references, route references, land/water classification, renderOrder, sourceLabel, and reviewNotes.
Compiled map candidates into deterministic runtime-m6-alpha-map-candidate-content-pack-v0 payloads with manifest counts, stable runtime IDs, route/district reference validation, and manifest hashing.

Added map-renderer createM6AlphaMapCandidateReadModelSnapshot as a presentational projection to existing ClientMapReadModelSnapshot shape; it uses local structural payload types to avoid adding a package dependency or lockfile change.
Kept exact borders, production art style, formal cultural symbols, complex naval warfare, 3D battle, manual node battle, and release map content out of scope.
risks: R3 boundary risk remains for systems_architect review: this touches content schema/runtime/tools and map-renderer read-model integration.
The map candidate is COMPOSITE/LOW confidence validation data; reviewNotes intentionally preserve uncertainty and do not lock final M7 map content.

map-renderer/tsconfig.json now enables allowImportingTsExtensions so it can typecheck workspace .ts exports already used through @monsoon/client-core; this is a TypeScript build-boundary adjustment, not runtime behavior.
requested_action: Review deterministic content/runtime/compiler boundaries, map-renderer presentational read-model projection, and acceptance evidence. Return ACCEPT or REQUEST_CHANGES; do not mark ACCEPTED until reviewer approval.
